com.mentor.chs.plugin.constraint

Interface IXAttributesResult


  • @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXAttributesResult
    Custom Constraint Return Object - Interface for returning attributes and properties to be set on object passed into the calling constraint.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addAttribute(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String value)
      Used in custom constraints to modify an attribute of an object.
      void addProperty(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String value)
      Used in custom constraints to add a proprty to an object.
      java.util.Collection<IXValue> getAttributes()
      Get a Collection of the attibutes on the object of this result
      java.util.Collection<IXValue> getProperties()
      Get a Collection of the properties on the object of this result

    • Method Detail

      • addAttribute

        void addAttribute(java.lang.String name,
                  java.lang.String value)
        Used in custom constraints to modify an attribute of an object. This replaces any previously added attribute of the same name with the new value. If the attribute doesn't exist on the object, no action is taken and no feedback is given.
        Parameters:
        name - - of attribute
        value - - of attribute (always a String)

      • addProperty

        void addProperty(java.lang.String name,
                 java.lang.String value)
        Used in custom constraints to add a proprty to an object. This replaces any previously added property of the same name with the new value. If the property doesn't exist on the object, a new property will be created.
        Parameters:
        name - - of property
        value - - of property (always a String)

      • getAttributes

        java.util.Collection<IXValue> getAttributes()
        Get a Collection of the attibutes on the object of this result
        Returns:
        a collection of the attributes on this result if any exist, an empty Collection otherwise.

      • getProperties

        java.util.Collection<IXValue> getProperties()
        Get a Collection of the properties on the object of this result
        Returns:
        a collection of the properties on this result if any exist, an empty Collection otherwise.
